title: Andy Burnham rules out paying compensation to Waspi women
description: Labour leadership hopeful says he does not support payments after backlash but is open to other benefits
content:
Andy Burnham has ruled out paying compensation to the “Waspi women” who claim they lost out owing to changes to the state pension age – but said he was open to the idea of giving them other benefits.

title: More than one in five pupils in England have special educational needs, figures show
description: Data shows sharp increase in number of children receiving extra support and highlights pressure on schools, families and councils
content:
More than one in five pupils in England now have special educational needs, as the latest official figures show a sharp increase in the numbers of children receiving extra support in school.
The annual data from the Department for Education (DfE) con... [3153 chars]

title: World Cup kicks off in Mexico with Shakira, performers and vibrant fans
description: A star-studded opening ceremony featured artists from the World Cup's official soundtrack.
content:
The 2026 World Cup has begun in Mexico City with an opening ceremony filled with colour, culture and a performance by global music star Shakira.
Thousands of fans arrived at the legendary Azteca stadium in Mexico City with a real sense of excitement ... [1744 chars]

title: Dozens of children put at risk after gender care failures at GP clinic, inquiry finds
description: A safety inquiry into the WellBN clinic in Brighton found 78 children were potentially harmed after being given gender medication without proper checks.
content:
Dozens of children questioning their gender, including some under 13 years old, were inappropriately prescribed medication by a Brighton GP practice, an NHS safety investigation has found.

title: Counter terrorism police leading Manchester school stabbing inquiry
description: Two pupils, both 14, and a teacher were hurt in the attack at a school in Manchester.
content:
Counter-terrorism police have taken over the investigation into a stabbing at a Manchester school in which two pupils and a teacher were injured.
The attack took place on Tuesday at the Co-op Academy on Plant Hill Road in the Blackley area.
